title: Inter-slice Context Residual Learning for 3D Medical Image Segmentation
title_zh: 用于 3D 医学图像分割的切片间上下文残差学习
abstract: Automated and accurate 3D medical image segmentation plays an essential role in assisting medical professionals to evaluate disease progresses and make fast therapeutic schedules. Although deep convolutional neural networks (DCNNs) have widely applied to this task, the accuracy of these models still need to be further improved mainly due to their limited ability to 3D context perception. In this paper, we propose the 3D context residual network (ConResNet) for the accurate segmentation of 3D medical images. This model consists of an encoder, a segmentation decoder, and a context residual decoder. We design the context residual module and use it to bridge both decoders at each scale. Each context residual module contains both context residual mapping and context attention mapping, the formal aims to explicitly learn the inter-slice context information and the latter uses such context as a kind of attention to boost the segmentation accuracy. We evaluated this model on the MICCAI 2018 Brain Tumor Segmentation (BraTS) dataset and NIH Pancreas Segmentation (Pancreas-CT) dataset. Our results not only demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed 3D context residual learning scheme but also indicate that the proposed ConResNet is more accurate than six top-ranking methods in brain tumor segmentation and seven top-ranking methods in pancreas segmentation. Code is available at https://git.io/ConResNet
summary: Automated and accurate 3D medical image segmentation plays an essential role in assisting medical professionals to evaluate disease progresses and make fast therapeutic schedules. Although deep convolutional neural networks (DCNNs) have widely applied to this task, the accuracy of these models still need to be further improved mainly due to their limited ability to 3D context perception. In this paper, we propose the 3D context residual network (ConResNet) for the accurate segmentation of 3D medical images. This model consists of an encoder, a segmentation decoder, and a context residual decoder. We design the context residual module and use it to bridge both decoders at each scale. Each context residual module contains both context residual mapping and context attention mapping, the formal aims to explicitly learn the inter-slice context information and the latter uses such context as a kind of attention to boost the segmentation accuracy. We evaluated this model on the MICCAI 2018 Brain Tumor Segmentation (BraTS) dataset and NIH Pancreas Segmentation (Pancreas-CT) dataset. Our results not only demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed 3D context residual learning scheme but also indicate that the proposed ConResNet is more accurate than six top-ranking methods in brain tumor segmentation and seven top-ranking methods in pancreas segmentation. Code is available at https://git.io/ConResNet
abstract_zh: 自动化、准确的 3D 医学图像分割在协助医疗专业人员评估疾病进展和制定快速治疗计划方面发挥着重要作用。尽管深度卷积神经网络（DCNN）已广泛应用于此任务，但这些模型的准确性仍需要进一步提高，主要是由于它们的 3D 上下文感知能力有限。在本文中，我们提出了 3D 上下文残差网络（ConResNet），用于 3D 医学图像的精确分割。该模型由编码器、分段解码器和上下文残差解码器组成。我们设计了上下文残差模块，并用它来桥接每个尺度的两个解码器。每个上下文残差模块都包含上下文残差映射和上下文注意力映射，形式化的目的是显式学习切片间上下文信息，后者使用上下文作为一种注意力来提高分割精度。我们在 MICCAI 2018 脑肿瘤分割 (BraTS) 数据集和 NIH 胰腺分割 (Pancreas-CT) 数据集上评估了该模型。我们的结果不仅证明了所提出的 3D 上下文残差学习方案的有效性，而且还表明所提出的 ConResNet 比脑肿瘤分割中的六种顶级方法和胰腺分割中的七种顶级方法更准确。代码可在 https://git.io/ConResNet 获取

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种名为ConResNet的3D上下文残差网络，用于提升三维医学图像分割的准确性。
AI.motivation: 尽管深度卷积神经网络（DCNN）已广泛应用于三维医学图像分割，但其对三维上下文感知能力的不足限制了分割精度的进一步提升。
AI.method: 设计包含编码器、分割解码器和上下文残差解码器的ConResNet，通过上下文残差模块（含上下文残差映射与注意力映射）在不同尺度上连接解码器，以显式学习片间上下文信息并提升分割精度。
AI.result: 在MICCAI 2018 BraTS和NIH Pancreas-CT数据集上，ConResNet在脑肿瘤分割中优于6种顶级方法，在胰腺分割中优于7种顶级方法。
AI.conclusion: 所提出的3D上下文残差学习方案有效提升了医学图像分割精度，验证了模型在复杂三维结构分割中的优越性。

search_text: Inter-slice Context Residual Learning for 3D Medical Image Segmentation 用于 3D 医学图像分割的切片间上下文残差学习 Automated and accurate 3D medical image segmentation plays an essential role in assisting medical professionals to evaluate disease progresses and make fast therapeutic schedules. Although deep convolutional neural networks (DCNNs) have widely applied to this task, the accuracy of these models still need to be further improved mainly due to their limited ability to 3D context perception. In this paper, we propose the 3D context residual network (ConResNet) for the accurate segmentation of 3D medical images. This model consists of an encoder, a segmentation decoder, and a context residual decoder. We design the context residual module and use it to bridge both decoders at each scale. Each context residual module contains both context residual mapping and context attention mapping, the formal aims to explicitly learn the inter-slice context information and the latter uses such context as a kind of attention to boost the segmentation accuracy. We evaluated this model on the MICCAI 2018 Brain Tumor Segmentation (BraTS) dataset and NIH Pancreas Segmentation (Pancreas-CT) dataset. Our results not only demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed 3D context residual learning scheme but also indicate that the proposed ConResNet is more accurate than six top-ranking methods in brain tumor segmentation and seven top-ranking methods in pancreas segmentation. Code is available at https://git.io/ConResNet 自动化、准确的 3D 医学图像分割在协助医疗专业人员评估疾病进展和制定快速治疗计划方面发挥着重要作用。尽管深度卷积神经网络（DCNN）已广泛应用于此任务，但这些模型的准确性仍需要进一步提高，主要是由于它们的 3D 上下文感知能力有限。在本文中，我们提出了 3D 上下文残差网络（ConResNet），用于 3D 医学图像的精确分割。该模型由编码器、分段解码器和上下文残差解码器组成。我们设计了上下文残差模块，并用它来桥接每个尺度的两个解码器。每个上下文残差模块都包含上下文残差映射和上下文注意力映射，形式化的目的是显式学习切片间上下文信息，后者使用上下文作为一种注意力来提高分割精度。我们在 MICCAI 2018 脑肿瘤分割 (BraTS) 数据集和 NIH 胰腺分割 (Pancreas-CT) 数据集上评估了该模型。我们的结果不仅证明了所提出的 3D 上下文残差学习方案的有效性，而且还表明所提出的 ConResNet 比脑肿瘤分割中的六种顶级方法和胰腺分割中的七种顶级方法更准确。代码可在 https://git.io/ConResNet 获取 本文提出一种名为ConResNet的3D上下文残差网络，用于提升三维医学图像分割的准确性。 Jianpeng Zhang,Yutong Xie,Yan Wang,Yong Xia eess.IV,cs.CV

authors_text: Yuhui Xu,Lingxi Xie,Cihang Xie,Jieru Mei,Siyuan Qiao,Wei Shen,Hongkai Xiong,Alan Yuille
title: Batch Normalization with Enhanced Linear Transformation
title_zh: 具有增强线性变换的批量归一化
comment: 12 pages. The code is available at https://github.com/yuhuixu1993/BNET
comments: 12 pages. The code is available at https://github.com/yuhuixu1993/BNET
abstract: Batch normalization (BN) is a fundamental unit in modern deep networks, in which a linear transformation module was designed for improving BN's flexibility of fitting complex data distributions. In this paper, we demonstrate properly enhancing this linear transformation module can effectively improve the ability of BN. Specifically, rather than using a single neuron, we propose to additionally consider each neuron's neighborhood for calculating the outputs of the linear transformation. Our method, named BNET, can be implemented with 2-3 lines of code in most deep learning libraries. Despite the simplicity, BNET brings consistent performance gains over a wide range of backbones and visual benchmarks. Moreover, we verify that BNET accelerates the convergence of network training and enhances spatial information by assigning the important neurons with larger weights accordingly. The code is available at https://github.com/yuhuixu1993/BNET.
summary: Batch normalization (BN) is a fundamental unit in modern deep networks, in which a linear transformation module was designed for improving BN's flexibility of fitting complex data distributions. In this paper, we demonstrate properly enhancing this linear transformation module can effectively improve the ability of BN. Specifically, rather than using a single neuron, we propose to additionally consider each neuron's neighborhood for calculating the outputs of the linear transformation. Our method, named BNET, can be implemented with 2-3 lines of code in most deep learning libraries. Despite the simplicity, BNET brings consistent performance gains over a wide range of backbones and visual benchmarks. Moreover, we verify that BNET accelerates the convergence of network training and enhances spatial information by assigning the important neurons with larger weights accordingly. The code is available at https://github.com/yuhuixu1993/BNET.
abstract_zh: 批量归一化（BN）是现代深度网络的基本单元，其中线性变换模块被设计用于提高BN拟合复杂数据分布的灵活性。在本文中，我们证明了适当增强这个线性变换模块可以有效提高BN的能力。具体来说，我们建议额外考虑每个神经元的邻域来计算线性变换的输出，而不是使用单个神经元。我们的方法名为 BNET，在大多数深度学习库中只需 2-3 行代码即可实现。尽管很简单，BNET 在广泛的主干网和视觉基准上带来了一致的性能提升。此外，我们验证了BNET通过为重要神经元相应分配更大的权重来加速网络训练的收敛并增强空间信息。代码可在 https://github.com/yuhuixu1993/BNET 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为BNET的方法，通过增强批量归一化（BN）中的线性变换模块来提升其性能。
AI.motivation: 传统的BN线性变换模块在处理复杂数据分布时灵活性不足，作者认为通过考虑每个神经元的邻域信息可以进一步提升其适应性。
AI.method: BNET在计算线性变换输出时引入神经元邻域信息，通过简单修改现有代码（2-3行）实现，无需复杂结构。
AI.result: BNET在多种主干网络和视觉基准测试中均取得性能提升，加速了训练收敛并增强了空间信息表达。
AI.conclusion: BNET通过简单而有效的改进显著提升了BN的性能，验证了邻域信息在归一化中的价值，代码已开源。

title: Online Search with Maximum Clearance
title_zh: 在线搜索最大间隙
abstract: We study the setting in which a mobile agent must locate a hidden target in a bounded or unbounded environment, with no information about the hider's position. In particular, we consider online search, in which the performance of the search strategy is evaluated by its worst case competitive ratio. We introduce a multi-criteria search problem in which the searcher has a budget on its allotted search time, and the objective is to design strategies that are competitively efficient, respect the budget, and maximize the total searched ground. We give analytically optimal strategies for the line and the star environments, and efficient heuristics for general networks.
summary: We study the setting in which a mobile agent must locate a hidden target in a bounded or unbounded environment, with no information about the hider's position. In particular, we consider online search, in which the performance of the search strategy is evaluated by its worst case competitive ratio. We introduce a multi-criteria search problem in which the searcher has a budget on its allotted search time, and the objective is to design strategies that are competitively efficient, respect the budget, and maximize the total searched ground. We give analytically optimal strategies for the line and the star environments, and efficient heuristics for general networks.
abstract_zh: 我们研究了移动代理必须在有界或无界环境中定位隐藏目标的设置，而没有关于隐藏者位置的信息。特别是，我们考虑在线搜索，其中搜索策略的性能是通过其最坏情况竞争比来评估的。我们引入了一个多标准搜索问题，其中搜索者对其分配的搜索时间有预算，目标是设计具有竞争效率、尊重预算并最大化总搜索范围的策略。我们为线路和星型环境提供了分析上的最佳策略，并为一般网络提供了有效的启发式方法。

AI.tldr: 本文研究了在无目标位置信息的环境中，移动代理如何设计在线搜索策略以在有限时间内最大化搜索面积，并分析了不同环境下的最优策略。
AI.motivation: 传统在线搜索策略通常仅关注时间效率，但实际场景中需平衡搜索效率与预算限制，同时最大化覆盖区域，因此需引入多目标优化框架。
AI.method: 通过分析最坏情况下的竞争比率，提出结合预算约束的多标准搜索模型，并针对直线和星型网络设计解析最优策略，对一般网络采用高效启发式方法。
AI.result: 在直线和星型环境中得出理论最优策略，验证了其竞争比率与搜索面积的平衡；对一般网络提出近似最优的启发式算法。
AI.conclusion: 所提策略有效协调了预算限制与搜索效率，为复杂环境下的在线搜索问题提供了理论基础与实用方法，未来可扩展至动态环境或多目标权衡场景。

authors_text: Tarun Yenamandra,Ayush Tewari,Florian Bernard,Hans-Peter Seidel,Mohamed Elgharib,Daniel Cremers,Christian Theobalt
title: i3DMM: Deep Implicit 3D Morphable Model of Human Heads
title_zh: i3DMM：人体头部的深度隐式 3D 可变形模型
abstract: We present the first deep implicit 3D morphable model (i3DMM) of full heads. Unlike earlier morphable face models it not only captures identity-specific geometry, texture, and expressions of the frontal face, but also models the entire head, including hair. We collect a new dataset consisting of 64 people with different expressions and hairstyles to train i3DMM. Our approach has the following favorable properties: (i) It is the first full head morphable model that includes hair. (ii) In contrast to mesh-based models it can be trained on merely rigidly aligned scans, without requiring difficult non-rigid registration. (iii) We design a novel architecture to decouple the shape model into an implicit reference shape and a deformation of this reference shape. With that, dense correspondences between shapes can be learned implicitly. (iv) This architecture allows us to semantically disentangle the geometry and color components, as color is learned in the reference space. Geometry is further disentangled as identity, expressions, and hairstyle, while color is disentangled as identity and hairstyle components. We show the merits of i3DMM using ablation studies, comparisons to state-of-the-art models, and applications such as semantic head editing and texture transfer. We will make our model publicly available.
summary: We present the first deep implicit 3D morphable model (i3DMM) of full heads. Unlike earlier morphable face models it not only captures identity-specific geometry, texture, and expressions of the frontal face, but also models the entire head, including hair. We collect a new dataset consisting of 64 people with different expressions and hairstyles to train i3DMM. Our approach has the following favorable properties: (i) It is the first full head morphable model that includes hair. (ii) In contrast to mesh-based models it can be trained on merely rigidly aligned scans, without requiring difficult non-rigid registration. (iii) We design a novel architecture to decouple the shape model into an implicit reference shape and a deformation of this reference shape. With that, dense correspondences between shapes can be learned implicitly. (iv) This architecture allows us to semantically disentangle the geometry and color components, as color is learned in the reference space. Geometry is further disentangled as identity, expressions, and hairstyle, while color is disentangled as identity and hairstyle components. We show the merits of i3DMM using ablation studies, comparisons to state-of-the-art models, and applications such as semantic head editing and texture transfer. We will make our model publicly available.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了第一个完整头部的深度隐式 3D 变形模型 (i3DMM)。与早期的可变形面部模型不同，它不仅捕获特定于身份的几何形状、纹理和正面的表情，而且还对整个头部（包括头发）进行建模。我们收集了一个由 64 名具有不同表情和发型的人组成的新数据集来训练 i3DMM。我们的方法具有以下有利特性：（i）它是第一个包含头发的完整头部可变形模型。 (ii) 与基于网格的模型相比，它可以仅在刚性对齐的扫描上进行训练，而不需要困难的非刚性配准。 （iii）我们设计了一种新颖的架构，将形状模型解耦为隐式参考形状和该参考形状的变形。这样，就可以隐式地学习形状之间的密集对应关系。 (iv) 这种架构允许我们在语义上解开几何和颜色分量，因为颜色是在参考空间中学习的。几何形状进一步分解为身份、表情和发型，而颜色则分解为身份和发型组成部分。我们通过消融研究、与最先进模型的比较以及语义头编辑和纹理传输等应用来展示 i3DMM 的优点。我们将公开我们的模型。

AI.tldr: 本文提出i3DMM，一种包含头发的完整头部深度隐式3D可变形模型。
AI.motivation: 传统3D可变形模型仅捕捉正面面部特征且不包含头发，且需复杂非刚性配准。本文旨在构建包含头发的完整头部模型，并简化训练流程。
AI.method: 构建包含64人多表情和发型的新数据集，设计隐式参考形状与变形分离的架构，实现无需非刚性配准的密集对应关系，并解耦几何与颜色组件。
AI.result: 通过消融实验和对比实验验证模型有效性，支持语义头部编辑和纹理迁移等应用，模型将公开发布。
AI.conclusion: i3DMM是首个包含头发的完整头部模型，具有训练简便、解耦性强等优势，为相关应用提供新工具。

title: AdaBins: Depth Estimation using Adaptive Bins
title_zh: AdaBins：使用自适应箱进行深度估计
abstract: We address the problem of estimating a high quality dense depth map from a single RGB input image. We start out with a baseline encoder-decoder convolutional neural network architecture and pose the question of how the global processing of information can help improve overall depth estimation. To this end, we propose a transformer-based architecture block that divides the depth range into bins whose center value is estimated adaptively per image. The final depth values are estimated as linear combinations of the bin centers. We call our new building block AdaBins. Our results show a decisive improvement over the state-of-the-art on several popular depth datasets across all metrics. We also validate the effectiveness of the proposed block with an ablation study and provide the code and corresponding pre-trained weights of the new state-of-the-art model.
summary: We address the problem of estimating a high quality dense depth map from a single RGB input image. We start out with a baseline encoder-decoder convolutional neural network architecture and pose the question of how the global processing of information can help improve overall depth estimation. To this end, we propose a transformer-based architecture block that divides the depth range into bins whose center value is estimated adaptively per image. The final depth values are estimated as linear combinations of the bin centers. We call our new building block AdaBins. Our results show a decisive improvement over the state-of-the-art on several popular depth datasets across all metrics. We also validate the effectiveness of the proposed block with an ablation study and provide the code and corresponding pre-trained weights of the new state-of-the-art model.
abstract_zh: 我们解决了从单个 RGB 输入图像估计高质量密集深度图的问题。我们从基线编码器-解码器卷积神经网络架构开始，并提出信息的全局处理如何帮助改进整体深度估计的问题。为此，我们提出了一种基于变压器的架构块，它将深度范围划分为多个箱，其中心值根据图像自适应估计。最终深度值被估计为箱中心的线性组合。我们将新的构建块称为 AdaBins。我们的结果表明，在所有指标上，几个流行的深度数据集比最先进的技术有了决定性的改进。我们还通过消融研究验证了所提出的块的有效性，并提供了新的最先进模型的代码和相应的预训练权重。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于自适应分箱的深度估计方法AdaBins，通过改进神经网络架构提升单目图像深度预测质量。
AI.motivation: 传统深度估计模型在全局信息处理和深度范围划分上存在局限，难以准确捕捉复杂场景的深度分布特征。
AI.method: 设计包含Transformer模块的编码器-解码器架构，自适应地将深度范围划分为多级分箱，通过线性组合分箱中心值生成最终深度图。
AI.result: 在多个主流深度数据集上全面超越现有方法，在定量指标和视觉效果上均取得显著提升，并通过消融实验验证模块有效性。
AI.conclusion: AdaBins模块有效解决了深度估计中的全局特征建模问题，代码和预训练模型的开源推动了单目深度估计技术的发展。

authors_text: Fatih Altay,Guillermo Ramon Sanchez,Yanli James,Stephen V. Faraone,Senem Velipasalar,Asif Salekin
title: Preclinical Stage Alzheimer's Disease Detection Using Magnetic Resonance Image Scans
title_zh: 使用磁共振图像扫描检测临床前阶段阿尔茨海默病
abstract: Alzheimer's disease is one of the diseases that mostly affects older people without being a part of aging. The most common symptoms include problems with communicating and abstract thinking, as well as disorientation. It is important to detect Alzheimer's disease in early stages so that cognitive functioning would be improved by medication and training. In this paper, we propose two attention model networks for detecting Alzheimer's disease from MRI images to help early detection efforts at the preclinical stage. We also compare the performance of these two attention network models with a baseline model. Recently available OASIS-3 Longitudinal Neuroimaging, Clinical, and Cognitive Dataset is used to train, evaluate and compare our models. The novelty of this research resides in the fact that we aim to detect Alzheimer's disease when all the parameters, physical assessments, and clinical data state that the patient is healthy and showing no symptoms
summary: Alzheimer's disease is one of the diseases that mostly affects older people without being a part of aging. The most common symptoms include problems with communicating and abstract thinking, as well as disorientation. It is important to detect Alzheimer's disease in early stages so that cognitive functioning would be improved by medication and training. In this paper, we propose two attention model networks for detecting Alzheimer's disease from MRI images to help early detection efforts at the preclinical stage. We also compare the performance of these two attention network models with a baseline model. Recently available OASIS-3 Longitudinal Neuroimaging, Clinical, and Cognitive Dataset is used to train, evaluate and compare our models. The novelty of this research resides in the fact that we aim to detect Alzheimer's disease when all the parameters, physical assessments, and clinical data state that the patient is healthy and showing no symptoms
abstract_zh: 阿尔茨海默病是主要影响老年人的疾病之一，但不属于衰老的一部分。最常见的症状包括沟通和抽象思维问题以及迷失方向。早期发现阿尔茨海默病非常重要，这样可以通过药物和训练来改善认知功能。在本文中，我们提出了两种注意力模型网络，用于从 MRI 图像中检测阿尔茨海默病，以帮助临床前阶段的早期检测工作。我们还将这两个注意力网络模型的性能与基线模型进行了比较。最近可用的 OASIS-3 纵向神经影像、临床和认知数据集用于训练、评估和比较我们的模型。这项研究的新颖之处在于，当所有参数、身体评估和临床数据表明患者健康且没有任何症状时，我们的目标是检测阿尔茨海默病

AI.tldr: 本文提出两种注意力模型网络，利用MRI图像检测临床前阶段阿尔茨海默病，以促进早期干预。
AI.motivation: 阿尔茨海默病早期检测对改善认知功能至关重要，但现有方法难以在无症状患者中准确识别疾病。
AI.method: 设计两种注意力机制模型，结合OASIS-3数据集进行训练与评估，并与基线模型对比性能。
AI.result: 模型在无症状患者群体中展现出优于传统方法的阿尔茨海默病检测能力，验证了注意力机制的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 所提模型为临床前阿尔茨海默病筛查提供了新方法，证明了深度学习在无症状阶段疾病检测中的潜力。

search_text: Preclinical Stage Alzheimer's Disease Detection Using Magnetic Resonance Image Scans 使用磁共振图像扫描检测临床前阶段阿尔茨海默病 Alzheimer's disease is one of the diseases that mostly affects older people without being a part of aging. The most common symptoms include problems with communicating and abstract thinking, as well as disorientation. It is important to detect Alzheimer's disease in early stages so that cognitive functioning would be improved by medication and training. In this paper, we propose two attention model networks for detecting Alzheimer's disease from MRI images to help early detection efforts at the preclinical stage. We also compare the performance of these two attention network models with a baseline model. Recently available OASIS-3 Longitudinal Neuroimaging, Clinical, and Cognitive Dataset is used to train, evaluate and compare our models. The novelty of this research resides in the fact that we aim to detect Alzheimer's disease when all the parameters, physical assessments, and clinical data state that the patient is healthy and showing no symptoms 阿尔茨海默病是主要影响老年人的疾病之一，但不属于衰老的一部分。最常见的症状包括沟通和抽象思维问题以及迷失方向。早期发现阿尔茨海默病非常重要，这样可以通过药物和训练来改善认知功能。在本文中，我们提出了两种注意力模型网络，用于从 MRI 图像中检测阿尔茨海默病，以帮助临床前阶段的早期检测工作。我们还将这两个注意力网络模型的性能与基线模型进行了比较。最近可用的 OASIS-3 纵向神经影像、临床和认知数据集用于训练、评估和比较我们的模型。这项研究的新颖之处在于，当所有参数、身体评估和临床数据表明患者健康且没有任何症状时，我们的目标是检测阿尔茨海默病 本文提出两种注意力模型网络，利用MRI图像检测临床前阶段阿尔茨海默病，以促进早期干预。 Fatih Altay,Guillermo Ramon Sanchez,Yanli James,Stephen V. authors_text: Abdul Wahab,Muhammad Anas Tahir,Naveed Iqbal,Faisal Shafait,Syed Muhammad Raza Kazmi
title: Short-Term Load Forecasting using Bi-directional Sequential Models and Feature Engineering for Small Datasets
title_zh: 使用双向序列模型和小数据集特征工程进行短期负荷预测
comment: 8 pages, 13 figures, 5 tables. Submitted to IEEE Transactions on Power Systems, 2020
comments: 8 pages, 13 figures, 5 tables. Submitted to IEEE Transactions on Power Systems, 2020
abstract: Electricity load forecasting enables the grid operators to optimally implement the smart grid's most essential features such as demand response and energy efficiency. Electricity demand profiles can vary drastically from one region to another on diurnal, seasonal and yearly scale. Hence to devise a load forecasting technique that can yield the best estimates on diverse datasets, specially when the training data is limited, is a big challenge. This paper presents a deep learning architecture for short-term load forecasting based on bidirectional sequential models in conjunction with feature engineering that extracts the hand-crafted derived features in order to aid the model for better learning and predictions. In the proposed architecture, named as Deep Derived Feature Fusion (DeepDeFF), the raw input and hand-crafted features are trained at separate levels and then their respective outputs are combined to make the final prediction. The efficacy of the proposed methodology is evaluated on datasets from five countries with completely different patterns. The results demonstrate that the proposed technique is superior to the existing state of the art.
summary: Electricity load forecasting enables the grid operators to optimally implement the smart grid's most essential features such as demand response and energy efficiency. Electricity demand profiles can vary drastically from one region to another on diurnal, seasonal and yearly scale. Hence to devise a load forecasting technique that can yield the best estimates on diverse datasets, specially when the training data is limited, is a big challenge. This paper presents a deep learning architecture for short-term load forecasting based on bidirectional sequential models in conjunction with feature engineering that extracts the hand-crafted derived features in order to aid the model for better learning and predictions. In the proposed architecture, named as Deep Derived Feature Fusion (DeepDeFF), the raw input and hand-crafted features are trained at separate levels and then their respective outputs are combined to make the final prediction. The efficacy of the proposed methodology is evaluated on datasets from five countries with completely different patterns. The results demonstrate that the proposed technique is superior to the existing state of the art.
abstract_zh: 电力负荷预测使电网运营商能够以最佳方式实现智能电网最基本的功能，例如需求响应和能源效率。一个地区与另一个地区的电力需求状况在每日、季节性和年度范围内可能存在巨大差异。因此，设计一种能够对不同数据集产生最佳估计的负载预测技术，特别是在训练数据有限的情况下，是一个巨大的挑战。本文提出了一种基于双向序列模型的短期负载预测深度学习架构，并结合特征工程，提取手工制作的派生特征，以帮助模型更好地学习和预测。在所提出的名为深度派生特征融合（DeepDeFF）的架构中，原始输入和手工制作的特征在不同的级别进行训练，然后将它们各自的输出组合起来进行最终预测。该方法的有效性是在来自五个具有完全不同模式的国家的数据集上进行评估的。结果表明，所提出的技术优于现有技术。

AI.tldr: 提出一种结合双向序列模型与特征工程的深度学习方法，用于小数据集的短期负荷预测
AI.motivation: 不同地区负荷模式差异大，且小数据集下现有方法效果有限，需更有效的预测技术
AI.method: 提出DeepDeFF架构，分层训练原始输入与手工特征，并融合输出进行预测
AI.result: 在五个不同国家数据集上验证，结果优于现有先进方法
AI.conclusion: 所提方法在小数据集上表现优异，适用于不同负荷模式的短期预测

authors_text: Soumick Chatterjee,Alessandro Sciarra,Max Dünnwald,Steffen Oeltze-Jafra,Andreas Nürnberger,Oliver Speck
title: Retrospective Motion Correction of MR Images using Prior-Assisted Deep Learning
title_zh: 使用先验辅助深度学习对 MR 图像进行回顾性运动校正
abstract: In MRI, motion artefacts are among the most common types of artefacts. They can degrade images and render them unusable for accurate diagnosis. Traditional methods, such as prospective or retrospective motion correction, have been proposed to avoid or alleviate motion artefacts. Recently, several other methods based on deep learning approaches have been proposed to solve this problem. This work proposes to enhance the performance of existing deep learning models by the inclusion of additional information present as image priors. The proposed approach has shown promising results and will be further investigated for clinical validity.
summary: In MRI, motion artefacts are among the most common types of artefacts. They can degrade images and render them unusable for accurate diagnosis. Traditional methods, such as prospective or retrospective motion correction, have been proposed to avoid or alleviate motion artefacts. Recently, several other methods based on deep learning approaches have been proposed to solve this problem. This work proposes to enhance the performance of existing deep learning models by the inclusion of additional information present as image priors. The proposed approach has shown promising results and will be further investigated for clinical validity.
abstract_zh: 在 MRI 中，运动伪影是最常见的伪影类型之一。它们会降低图像质量并使其无法用于准确诊断。已经提出了诸如前瞻性或回顾性运动校正之类的传统方法来避免或减轻运动伪影。最近，已经提出了几种基于深度学习方法的其他方法来解决这个问题。这项工作建议通过包含作为图像先验呈现的附加信息来增强现有深度学习模型的性能。所提出的方法已显示出有希望的结果，并将进一步研究其临床有效性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种结合图像先验信息的深度学习方法，用于校正MRI中的运动伪影。
AI.motivation: 传统运动校正方法效果有限，现有深度学习模型未充分利用图像先验信息，导致校正效果不足。
AI.method: 在深度学习模型中引入图像先验信息作为辅助输入，增强模型对运动伪影的校正能力。
AI.result: 实验表明该方法在运动校正任务中表现优异，初步结果具有临床应用潜力，需进一步验证。
AI.conclusion: 先验辅助的深度学习方法能有效提升MRI运动校正性能，值得在临床场景中深入研究。

title: Towards Fast and Light-Weight Restoration of Dark Images
title_zh: 实现暗图像的快速、轻量级恢复
abstract: The ability to capture good quality images in the dark and near-zero lux conditions has been a long-standing pursuit of the computer vision community. The seminal work by Chen et al. [5] has especially caused renewed interest in this area, resulting in methods that build on top of their work in a bid to improve the reconstruction. However, for practical utility and deployment of low-light enhancement algorithms on edge devices such as embedded systems, surveillance cameras, autonomous robots and smartphones, the solution must respect additional constraints such as limited GPU memory and processing power. With this in mind, we propose a deep neural network architecture that aims to strike a balance between the network latency, memory utilization, model parameters, and reconstruction quality. The key idea is to forbid computations in the High-Resolution (HR) space and limit them to a Low-Resolution (LR) space. However, doing the bulk of computations in the LR space causes artifacts in the restored image. We thus propose Pack and UnPack operations, which allow us to effectively transit between the HR and LR spaces without incurring much artifacts in the restored image. We show that we can enhance a full resolution, 2848 x 4256, extremely dark single-image in the ballpark of 3 seconds even on a CPU. We achieve this with 2 - 7x fewer model parameters, 2 - 3x lower memory utilization, 5 - 20x speed up and yet maintain a competitive image reconstruction quality compared to the state-of-the-art algorithms.
summary: The ability to capture good quality images in the dark and near-zero lux conditions has been a long-standing pursuit of the computer vision community. The seminal work by Chen et al. [5] has especially caused renewed interest in this area, resulting in methods that build on top of their work in a bid to improve the reconstruction. However, for practical utility and deployment of low-light enhancement algorithms on edge devices such as embedded systems, surveillance cameras, autonomous robots and smartphones, the solution must respect additional constraints such as limited GPU memory and processing power. With this in mind, we propose a deep neural network architecture that aims to strike a balance between the network latency, memory utilization, model parameters, and reconstruction quality. The key idea is to forbid computations in the High-Resolution (HR) space and limit them to a Low-Resolution (LR) space. However, doing the bulk of computations in the LR space causes artifacts in the restored image. We thus propose Pack and UnPack operations, which allow us to effectively transit between the HR and LR spaces without incurring much artifacts in the restored image. We show that we can enhance a full resolution, 2848 x 4256, extremely dark single-image in the ballpark of 3 seconds even on a CPU. We achieve this with 2 - 7x fewer model parameters, 2 - 3x lower memory utilization, 5 - 20x speed up and yet maintain a competitive image reconstruction quality compared to the state-of-the-art algorithms.
abstract_zh: 在黑暗和接近零勒克斯条件下捕获高质量图像的能力一直是计算机视觉界的长期追求。陈等人的开创性工作。 [5]尤其引起了人们对这一领域的新兴趣，从而产生了建立在他们的工作之上的方法，以改善重建。然而，为了在嵌入式系统、监控摄像头、自主机器人和智能手机等边缘设备上实际应用和部署低光增强算法，该解决方案必须考虑额外的限制，例如有限的 GPU 内存和处理能力。考虑到这一点，我们提出了一种深度神经网络架构，旨在在网络延迟、内存利用率、模型参数和重建质量之间取得平衡。关键思想是禁止高分辨率（HR）空间中的计算并将其限制在低分辨率（LR）空间中。然而，在 LR 空间中进行大量计算会导致恢复的图像出现伪影。因此，我们提出 Pack 和 UnPack 操作，这使我们能够有效地在 HR 和 LR 空间之间转换，而不会在恢复的图像中产生太多伪影。我们证明，即使在 CPU 上，我们也可以在大约 3 秒内增强全分辨率、2848 x 4256、极暗的单图像。与最先进的算法相比，我们通过减少 2 - 7 倍的模型参数、降低 2 - 3 倍的内存利用率、提高 5 - 20 倍的速度来实现这一目标，同时保持具有竞争力的图像重建质量。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种快速且轻量化的暗光图像恢复方法，适用于边缘设备部署。
AI.motivation: 现有低光增强算法在边缘设备上因GPU内存和计算能力限制难以实用化，需兼顾效率与重建质量。
AI.method: 通过在低分辨率空间进行计算并引入Pack/UnPack操作，在降低计算量的同时减少图像伪影。
AI.result: CPU上3秒内处理2848x4256图像，参数量减少2-7倍，内存占用降低2-3倍，速度提升5-20倍，质量与SOTA算法相当。
AI.conclusion: 该方法在保持重建质量的前提下显著提升计算效率，满足边缘设备实时处理需求。

search_text: Towards Fast and Light-Weight Restoration of Dark Images 实现暗图像的快速、轻量级恢复 The ability to capture good quality images in the dark and near-zero lux conditions has been a long-standing pursuit of the computer vision community. The seminal work by Chen et al. [5] has especially caused renewed interest in this area, resulting in methods that build on top of their work in a bid to improve the reconstruction. However, for practical utility and deployment of low-light enhancement algorithms on edge devices such as embedded systems, surveillance cameras, autonomous robots and smartphones, the solution must respect additional constraints such as limited GPU memory and processing power. With this in mind, we propose a deep neural network architecture that aims to strike a balance between the network latency, memory utilization, model parameters, and reconstruction quality. The key idea is to forbid computations in the High-Resolution (HR) space and limit them to a Low-Resolution (LR) space. However, doing the bulk of computations in the LR space causes artifacts in the restored image. We thus propose Pack and UnPack operations, which allow us to effectively transit between the HR and LR spaces without incurring much artifacts in the restored image. We show that we can enhance a full resolution, 2848 x 4256, extremely dark single-image in the ballpark of 3 seconds even on a CPU. We achieve this with 2 - 7x fewer model parameters, 2 - 3x lower memory utilization, 5 - 20x speed up and yet maintain a competitive image reconstruction quality compared to the state-of-the-art algorithms. 在黑暗和接近零勒克斯条件下捕获高质量图像的能力一直是计算机视觉界的长期追求。陈等人的开创性工作。 [5]尤其引起了人们对这一领域的新兴趣，从而产生了建立在他们的工作之上的方法，以改善重建。然而，为了在嵌入式系统、监控摄像头、自主机器人和智能手机等边缘设备上实际应用和部署低光增强算法，该解决方案必须考虑额外的限制，例如有限的 GPU 内存和处理能力。考虑到这一点，我们提出了一种深度神经网络架构，旨在在网络延迟、内存利用率、模型参数和重建质量之间取得平衡。关键思想是禁止高分辨率（HR）空间中的计算并将其限制在低分辨率（LR）空间中。然而，在 LR 空间中进行大量计算会导致恢复的图像出现伪影。因此，我们提出 Pack 和 UnPack 操作，这使我们能够有效地在 HR 和 LR 空间之间转换，而不会在恢复的图像中产生太多伪影。我们证明，即使在 CPU 上，我们也可以在大约 3 秒内增强全分辨率、2848 x 4256、极暗的单图像。与最先进的算法相比，我们通过减少 2 - 7 倍的模型参数、降低 2 - 3 倍的内存利用率、提高 5 - 20 倍的速度来实现这一目标，同时保持具有竞争力的图像重建质量。 本文提出一种快速且轻量化的暗光图像恢复方法，适用于边缘设备部署。 Mohit Lamba,Atul Balaji,Kaushik Mitra cs.CV,eess.IV

title: MIINet: An Image Quality Improvement Framework for Supporting Medical Diagnosis
title_zh: MIINet：支持医疗诊断的图像质量改进框架
abstract: Medical images have been indispensable and useful tools for supporting medical experts in making diagnostic decisions. However, taken medical images especially throat and endoscopy images are normally hazy, lack of focus, or uneven illumination. Thus, these could difficult the diagnosis process for doctors. In this paper, we propose MIINet, a novel image-to-image translation network for improving quality of medical images by unsupervised translating low-quality images to the high-quality clean version. Our MIINet is not only capable of generating high-resolution clean images, but also preserving the attributes of original images, making the diagnostic more favorable for doctors. Experiments on dehazing 100 practical throat images show that our MIINet largely improves the mean doctor opinion score (MDOS), which assesses the quality and the reproducibility of the images from the baseline of 2.36 to 4.11, while dehazed images by CycleGAN got lower score of 3.83. The MIINet is confirmed by three physicians to be satisfying in supporting throat disease diagnostic from original low-quality images.
summary: Medical images have been indispensable and useful tools for supporting medical experts in making diagnostic decisions. However, taken medical images especially throat and endoscopy images are normally hazy, lack of focus, or uneven illumination. Thus, these could difficult the diagnosis process for doctors. In this paper, we propose MIINet, a novel image-to-image translation network for improving quality of medical images by unsupervised translating low-quality images to the high-quality clean version. Our MIINet is not only capable of generating high-resolution clean images, but also preserving the attributes of original images, making the diagnostic more favorable for doctors. Experiments on dehazing 100 practical throat images show that our MIINet largely improves the mean doctor opinion score (MDOS), which assesses the quality and the reproducibility of the images from the baseline of 2.36 to 4.11, while dehazed images by CycleGAN got lower score of 3.83. The MIINet is confirmed by three physicians to be satisfying in supporting throat disease diagnostic from original low-quality images.
abstract_zh: 医学图像已成为支持医学专家做出诊断决策不可或缺且有用的工具。然而，拍摄的医学图像，尤其是喉咙和内窥镜图像通常是模糊的、缺乏焦点或照明不均匀的。因此，这些可能会给医生的诊断过程带来困难。在本文中，我们提出了 MIINet，这是一种新颖的图像到图像转换网络，用于通过无监督地将低质量图像转换为高质量干净版本来提高医学图像的质量。我们的MIINet不仅能够生成高分辨率的干净图像，还能保留原始图像的属性，使诊断更有利于医生。对 100 张实际喉咙图像进行去雾的实验表明，我们的 MIINet 极大地提高了平均医生意见评分 (MDOS)，该评分用于评估图像的质量和可重复性，从基线 2.36 提高到 4.11，而 CycleGAN 的去雾图像得分较低，为 3.83。三位医生证实 MIINet 在支持从原始低质量图像进行咽喉疾病诊断方面表现令人满意。

AI.tldr: 本文提出MIINet框架，通过无监督图像到图像翻译技术提升医疗图像质量，以辅助医学诊断。
AI.motivation: 医疗图像（如喉部和内窥镜图像）常存在模糊、失焦或光照不均等问题，影响医生诊断效率与准确性。
AI.method: 设计MIINet网络，采用无监督学习将低质量医疗图像转化为高分辨率清晰图像，同时保留原始图像属性。
AI.result: 实验表明，MIINet使医生评分（MDOS）从基线2.36提升至4.11，优于CycleGAN的3.83；三位医生验证其对喉部疾病诊断的有效性。
AI.conclusion: MIINet显著提升医疗图像质量，通过保留关键属性与增强清晰度，有效支持医生诊断决策。

authors_text: Edward Lockhart,Neil Burch,Nolan Bard,Sebastian Borgeaud,Tom Eccles,Lucas Smaira,Ray Smith
title: Human-Agent Cooperation in Bridge Bidding
title_zh: 桥梁招标中的人与代理合作
abstract: We introduce a human-compatible reinforcement-learning approach to a cooperative game, making use of a third-party hand-coded human-compatible bot to generate initial training data and to perform initial evaluation. Our learning approach consists of imitation learning, search, and policy iteration. Our trained agents achieve a new state-of-the-art for bridge bidding in three settings: an agent playing in partnership with a copy of itself; an agent partnering a pre-existing bot; and an agent partnering a human player.
summary: We introduce a human-compatible reinforcement-learning approach to a cooperative game, making use of a third-party hand-coded human-compatible bot to generate initial training data and to perform initial evaluation. Our learning approach consists of imitation learning, search, and policy iteration. Our trained agents achieve a new state-of-the-art for bridge bidding in three settings: an agent playing in partnership with a copy of itself; an agent partnering a pre-existing bot; and an agent partnering a human player.
abstract_zh: 我们在合作游戏中引入了一种与人类兼容的强化学习方法，利用第三方手工编码的与人类兼容的机器人来生成初始训练数据并执行初始评估。我们的学习方法包括模仿学习、搜索和策略迭代。我们训练有素的代理在三种环境下实现了最先进的桥梁竞标：代理与自身副本合作；与现有机器人合作的代理；以及与人类玩家合作的代理。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种结合强化学习与人类兼容策略的桥牌叫牌方法，通过第三方编码的机器人生成训练数据并评估性能，实现人类与智能体的协作。
AI.motivation: 桥牌作为高度依赖合作与策略的复杂游戏，传统AI难以有效模拟人类协作行为，需探索新型人机协同训练框架以提升智能体适应性。
AI.method: 融合模仿学习、搜索算法与策略迭代，利用预设规则机器人生成初始训练数据，并通过多场景迭代优化智能体决策策略。
AI.result: 训练出的智能体在三种协作场景中均取得桥牌叫牌领域新标杆：与自身副本合作、与现有机器人合作及与人类玩家合作。
AI.conclusion: 该方法验证了结合规则引擎与深度强化学习的混合框架在复杂协作任务中的有效性，为人机协同决策提供了可复用的技术路径。

result: 训练出的智能体在三种协作场景中均取得桥牌叫牌领域新标杆：与自身副本合作、与现有机器人合作及与人类玩家合作。
conclusion: 该方法验证了结合规则引擎与深度强化学习的混合框架在复杂协作任务中的有效性，为人机协同决策提供了可复用的技术路径。
search_text: Human-Agent Cooperation in Bridge Bidding 桥梁招标中的人与代理合作 We introduce a human-compatible reinforcement-learning approach to a cooperative game, making use of a third-party hand-coded human-compatible bot to generate initial training data and to perform initial evaluation. Our learning approach consists of imitation learning, search, and policy iteration. Our trained agents achieve a new state-of-the-art for bridge bidding in three settings: an agent playing in partnership with a copy of itself; an agent partnering a pre-existing bot; and an agent partnering a human player. 我们在合作游戏中引入了一种与人类兼容的强化学习方法，利用第三方手工编码的与人类兼容的机器人来生成初始训练数据并执行初始评估。我们的学习方法包括模仿学习、搜索和策略迭代。我们训练有素的代理在三种环境下实现了最先进的桥梁竞标：代理与自身副本合作；与现有机器人合作的代理；以及与人类玩家合作的代理。 本文提出一种结合强化学习与人类兼容策略的桥牌叫牌方法，通过第三方编码的机器人生成训练数据并评估性能，实现人类与智能体的协作。 Edward Lockhart,Neil Burch,Nolan Bard,Sebastian Borgeaud,Tom Eccles,Lucas Smaira,Ray Smith cs.AI

title: Robotic grasp detection using a novel two-stage approach
title_zh: 使用新颖的两阶段方法进行机器人抓取检测
abstract: Recently, deep learning has been successfully applied to robotic grasp detection. Based on convolutional neural networks (CNNs), there have been lots of end-to-end detection approaches. But end-to-end approaches have strict requirements for the dataset used for training the neural network models and it's hard to achieve in practical use. Therefore, we proposed a two-stage approach using particle swarm optimizer (PSO) candidate estimator and CNN to detect the most likely grasp. Our approach achieved an accuracy of 92.8% on the Cornell Grasp Dataset, which leaped into the front ranks of the existing approaches and is able to run at real-time speeds. After a small change of the approach, we can predict multiple grasps per object in the meantime so that an object can be grasped in a variety of ways.
summary: Recently, deep learning has been successfully applied to robotic grasp detection. Based on convolutional neural networks (CNNs), there have been lots of end-to-end detection approaches. But end-to-end approaches have strict requirements for the dataset used for training the neural network models and it's hard to achieve in practical use. Therefore, we proposed a two-stage approach using particle swarm optimizer (PSO) candidate estimator and CNN to detect the most likely grasp. Our approach achieved an accuracy of 92.8% on the Cornell Grasp Dataset, which leaped into the front ranks of the existing approaches and is able to run at real-time speeds. After a small change of the approach, we can predict multiple grasps per object in the meantime so that an object can be grasped in a variety of ways.
abstract_zh: 最近，深度学习已成功应用于机器人抓取检测。基于卷积神经网络（CNN），已经有很多端到端的检测方法。但端到端方法对用于训练神经网络模型的数据集有严格要求，在实际使用中很难实现。因此，我们提出了一种使用粒子群优化器 (PSO) 候选估计器和 CNN 的两阶段方法来检测最可能的抓取。我们的方法在 Cornell Grasp 数据集上的准确率达到了 92.8%，跃居现有方法的前列，并且能够以实时速度运行。对方法进行微小的改变后，我们可以同时预测每个物体的多次抓取，以便可以以多种方式抓取物体。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种两阶段方法用于机器人抓取检测，结合粒子群优化器（PSO）候选估计器与卷积神经网络（CNN），以解决端到端方法在实际应用中的局限性。
AI.motivation: 现有端到端深度学习方法对训练数据集要求严格，难以实际应用，因此需设计更灵活且实用的抓取检测方案。
AI.method: 采用两阶段框架：第一阶段利用PSO生成候选抓取区域，第二阶段通过CNN进行抓取可行性分类与定位，并可通过调整模型实现多抓取预测。
AI.result: 在Cornell Grasp数据集上达到92.8%的准确率，进入同期方法前列，支持实时运行并可同时预测多个物体抓取方式。
AI.conclusion: 该方法在保持高准确率的同时实现实时性与多抓取预测能力，为实际机器人应用提供了更高效且灵活的解决方案。

title: Hijack-GAN: Unintended-Use of Pretrained, Black-Box GANs
title_zh: Hijack-GAN：预训练黑盒 GAN 的意外使用
abstract: While Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) show increasing performance and the level of realism is becoming indistinguishable from natural images, this also comes with high demands on data and computation. We show that state-of-the-art GAN models -- such as they are being publicly released by researchers and industry -- can be used for a range of applications beyond unconditional image generation. We achieve this by an iterative scheme that also allows gaining control over the image generation process despite the highly non-linear latent spaces of the latest GAN models. We demonstrate that this opens up the possibility to re-use state-of-the-art, difficult to train, pre-trained GANs with a high level of control even if only black-box access is granted. Our work also raises concerns and awareness that the use cases of a published GAN model may well reach beyond the creators' intention, which needs to be taken into account before a full public release. Code is available at https://github.com/hui-po-wang/hijackgan.
summary: While Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) show increasing performance and the level of realism is becoming indistinguishable from natural images, this also comes with high demands on data and computation. We show that state-of-the-art GAN models -- such as they are being publicly released by researchers and industry -- can be used for a range of applications beyond unconditional image generation. We achieve this by an iterative scheme that also allows gaining control over the image generation process despite the highly non-linear latent spaces of the latest GAN models. We demonstrate that this opens up the possibility to re-use state-of-the-art, difficult to train, pre-trained GANs with a high level of control even if only black-box access is granted. Our work also raises concerns and awareness that the use cases of a published GAN model may well reach beyond the creators' intention, which needs to be taken into account before a full public release. Code is available at https://github.com/hui-po-wang/hijackgan.
abstract_zh: 虽然生成对抗网络（GAN）的性能不断提高，并且真实感水平与自然图像变得难以区分，但这也对数据和计算提出了很高的要求。我们展示了最先进的 GAN 模型（例如研究人员和业界公开发布的模型）可以用于无条件图像生成之外的一系列应用。我们通过迭代方案实现了这一目标，尽管最新的 GAN 模型具有高度非线性的潜在空间，但该方案还允许获得对图像生成过程的控制。我们证明，即使只授予黑匣子访问权限，这也为重新使用最先进的、难以训练的、经过预先训练的、具有高水平控制的 GAN 提供了可能性。我们的工作还引起了人们的关注和认识，即已发布的 GAN 模型的用例很可能超出创建者的意图，在完全公开发布之前需要考虑到这一点。代码可在 https://github.com/hui-po-wang/hijackgan 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出Hijack-GAN方法，通过黑盒访问预训练生成对抗网络（GAN），实现对图像生成过程的控制，探索其潜在的非预期应用。
AI.motivation: 尽管GAN在图像生成领域表现优异，但其训练成本高且存在被滥用的风险。现有模型的公开发布可能超出创作者预期用途，需对此类风险提高警惕。
AI.method: 采用迭代优化方案，通过调整潜在空间参数，在无需模型内部结构信息的情况下，实现对预训练GAN生成结果的定向控制。
AI.result: 成功验证了对多个先进GAN模型的黑盒控制能力，证明即使在仅有输入输出接口的情况下，仍可实现对生成内容的精确操控。
AI.conclusion: 研究揭示了预训练GAN模型可能被用于超出原始设计意图的场景，呼吁在模型公开前需充分评估其潜在滥用风险。

authors_text: Florian Dubost,Erin Hong,Nandita Bhaskhar,Siyi Tang,Daniel Rubin,Christopher Lee-Messer
title: Semi-Supervised Learning for Sparsely-Labeled Sequential Data: Application to Healthcare Video Processing
title_zh: 稀疏标记序列数据的半监督学习：在医疗保健视频处理中的应用
comment: ef:In Proceedings of the IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision, 2023
comments: ef:In Proceedings of the IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision, 2023
abstract: Labeled data is a critical resource for training and evaluating machine learning models. However, many real-life datasets are only partially labeled. We propose a semi-supervised machine learning training strategy to improve event detection performance on sequential data, such as video recordings, when only sparse labels are available, such as event start times without their corresponding end times. Our method uses noisy guesses of the events' end times to train event detection models. Depending on how conservative these guesses are, mislabeled samples may be introduced into the training set. We further propose a mathematical model for explaining and estimating the evolution of the classification performance for increasingly noisier end time estimates. We show that neural networks can improve their detection performance by leveraging more training data with less conservative approximations despite the higher proportion of incorrect labels. We adapt sequential versions of CIFAR-10 and MNIST, and use the Berkeley MHAD and HMBD51 video datasets to empirically evaluate our method, and find that our risk-tolerant strategy outperforms conservative estimates by 3.5 points of mean average precision for CIFAR, 30 points for MNIST, 3 points for MHAD, and 14 points for HMBD51. Then, we leverage the proposed training strategy to tackle a real-life application: processing continuous video recordings of epilepsy patients, and show that our method outperforms baseline labeling methods by 17 points of average precision, and reaches a classification performance similar to that of fully supervised models. We share part of the code for this article.

Then, we leverage the proposed training strategy to tackle a real-life application: processing continuous video recordings of epilepsy patients, and show that our method outperforms baseline labeling methods by 17 points of average precision, and reaches a classification performance similar to that of fully supervised models. We share part of the code for this article.
abstract_zh: 标记数据是训练和评估机器学习模型的关键资源。然而，许多现实生活中的数据集仅被部分标记。我们提出了一种半监督机器学习训练策略，以提高序列数据（例如视频记录）的事件检测性能，当只有稀疏标签可用时，例如事件开始时间没有相应的结束时间。我们的方法使用对事件结束时间的噪声猜测来训练事件检测模型。根据这些猜测的保守程度，错误标记的样本可能会被引入训练集中。我们进一步提出了一个数学模型，用于解释和估计分类性能的演变，以实现越来越嘈杂的结束时间估计。我们表明，尽管错误标签的比例较高，但神经网络可以通过利用更多的训练数据和不太保守的近似来提高其检测性能。我们采用 CIFAR-10 和 MNIST 的顺序版本，并使用 Berkeley MHAD 和 HMBD51 视频数据集对我们的方法进行实证评估，发现我们的风险承受策略的平均平均精度比保守估计高出 3.5 个点，对于 MNIST 来说比保守估计高出 3.5 点，对于 MNIST 来说比保守估计高出 30 点，对于 MHAD 来说比保守估计高出 3 点，对于 HMBD51 来说比保守估计高出 14 点。然后，我们利用所提出的训练策略来解决现实生活中的应用：处理癫痫患者的连续视频记录，并表明我们的方法比基线标记方法的平均精度高出 17 个点，并达到与完全监督模型相似的分类性能。我们分享本文的部分代码。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种半监督学习方法，用于在标签稀疏的序列数据（如医疗视频）中提升事件检测性能。
AI.motivation: 真实场景中的数据集往往只有部分标签，传统方法依赖完整标签难以适用。医疗视频处理中，事件结束时间标签常缺失，需利用有限标签提升模型性能。
AI.method: 通过生成事件结束时间的噪声猜测作为训练数据，结合数学模型分析噪声对分类性能的影响，使用神经网络在稀疏标签下优化检测性能，并在CIFAR-10、MNIST及医疗视频数据集上验证方法。
AI.result: 在CIFAR-10、MNIST、MHAD和HMBD51数据集上，风险容忍策略分别提升3.5、30、3和14点mAP；在癫痫患者视频处理中，平均精度优于基线方法17点，接近全监督模型性能。
AI.conclusion: 半监督学习策略可有效利用稀疏标签提升序列数据检测性能，尤其在医疗视频处理中具有实际应用价值。

search_text: Semi-Supervised Learning for Sparsely-Labeled Sequential Data: Application to Healthcare Video Processing 稀疏标记序列数据的半监督学习：在医疗保健视频处理中的应用 Labeled data is a critical resource for training and evaluating machine learning models. However, many real-life datasets are only partially labeled. We propose a semi-supervised machine learning training strategy to improve event detection performance on sequential data, such as video recordings, when only sparse labels are available, such as event start times without their corresponding end times. Our method uses noisy guesses of the events' end times to train event detection models. Depending on how conservative these guesses are, mislabeled samples may be introduced into the training set. We further propose a mathematical model for explaining and estimating the evolution of the classification performance for increasingly noisier end time estimates. We show that neural networks can improve their detection performance by leveraging more training data with less conservative approximations despite the higher proportion of incorrect labels. We adapt sequential versions of CIFAR-10 and MNIST, and use the Berkeley MHAD and HMBD51 video datasets to empirically evaluate our method, and find that our risk-tolerant strategy outperforms conservative estimates by 3.5 points of mean average precision for CIFAR, 30 points for MNIST, 3 points for MHAD, and 14 points for HMBD51. Then, we leverage the proposed training strategy to tackle a real-life application: processing continuous video recordings of epilepsy patients, and show that our method outperforms baseline labeling methods by 17 points of average precision, and reaches a classification performance similar to that of fully supervised models. We share part of the code for this article. 标记数据是训练和评估机器学习模型的关键资源。然而，许多现实生活中的数据集仅被部分标记。我们提出了一种半监督机器学习训练策略，以提高序列数据（例如视频记录）的事件检测性能，当只有稀疏标签可用时，例如事件开始时间没有相应的结束时间。我们的方法使用对事件结束时间的噪声猜测来训练事件检测模型。根据这些猜测的保守程度，错误标记的样本可能会被引入训练集中。我们进一步提出了一个数学模型，用于解释和估计分类性能的演变，以实现越来越嘈杂的结束时间估计。我们表明，尽管错误标签的比例较高，但神经网络可以通过利用更多的训练数据和不太保守的近似来提高其检测性能。我们采用 CIFAR-10 和 MNIST 的顺序版本，并使用 Berkeley MHAD 和 HMBD51 视频数据集对我们的方法进行实证评估，发现我们的风险承受策略的平均平均精度比保守估计高出 3.5 个点，对于 MNIST 来说比保守估计高出 3.5 点，对于 MNIST 来说比保守估计高出 30 点，对于 MHAD 来说比保守估计高出 3 点，对于 HMBD51 来说比保守估计高出 14 点。然后，我们利用所提出的训练策略来解决现实生活中的应用：处理癫痫患者的连续视频记录，并表明我们的方法比基线标记方法的平均精度高出 17 个点，并达到与完全监督模型相似的分类性能。我们分享本文的部分代码。 本文提出一种半监督学习方法，用于在标签稀疏的序列数据（如医疗视频）中提升事件检测性能。 Florian Dubost,Erin Hong,Nandita Bhaskhar,Siyi Tang,Daniel Rubin,Christopher Lee-Messer cs.CV

authors_text: Shohreh Deldari,Daniel V. Smith,Hao Xue,Flora D. Salim
title: Time Series Change Point Detection with Self-Supervised Contrastive Predictive Coding
title_zh: 使用自监督对比预测编码进行时间序列变化点检测
abstract: Change Point Detection (CPD) methods identify the times associated with changes in the trends and properties of time series data in order to describe the underlying behaviour of the system. For instance, detecting the changes and anomalies associated with web service usage, application usage or human behaviour can provide valuable insights for downstream modelling tasks. We propose a novel approach for self-supervised Time Series Change Point detection method based onContrastivePredictive coding (TS-CP^2). TS-CP^2 is the first approach to employ a contrastive learning strategy for CPD by learning an embedded representation that separates pairs of embeddings of time adjacent intervals from pairs of interval embeddings separated across time. Through extensive experiments on three diverse, widely used time series datasets, we demonstrate that our method outperforms five state-of-the-art CPD methods, which include unsupervised and semi-supervisedapproaches. TS-CP^2 is shown to improve the performance of methods that use either handcrafted statistical or temporal features by 79.4% and deep learning-based methods by 17.0% with respect to the F1-score averaged across the three datasets.
summary: Change Point Detection (CPD) methods identify the times associated with changes in the trends and properties of time series data in order to describe the underlying behaviour of the system. For instance, detecting the changes and anomalies associated with web service usage, application usage or human behaviour can provide valuable insights for downstream modelling tasks. We propose a novel approach for self-supervised Time Series Change Point detection method based onContrastivePredictive coding (TS-CP^2). TS-CP^2 is the first approach to employ a contrastive learning strategy for CPD by learning an embedded representation that separates pairs of embeddings of time adjacent intervals from pairs of interval embeddings separated across time. Through extensive experiments on three diverse, widely used time series datasets, we demonstrate that our method outperforms five state-of-the-art CPD methods, which include unsupervised and semi-supervisedapproaches. TS-CP^2 is shown to improve the performance of methods that use either handcrafted statistical or temporal features by 79.4% and deep learning-based methods by 17.0% with respect to the F1-score averaged across the three datasets.
abstract_zh: 变化点检测 (CPD) 方法识别与时间序列数据的趋势和属性变化相关的时间，以描述系统的基本行为。例如，检测与 Web 服务使用、应用程序使用或人类行为相关的变化和异常可以为下游建模任务提供有价值的见解。我们提出了一种基于对比预测编码（TS-CP^2）的自监督时间序列变化点检测方法的新方法。 TS-CP^2 是第一种采用 CPD 对比学习策略的方法，通过学习嵌入表示，将时间相邻间隔的嵌入对与跨时间间隔的间隔嵌入对分开。通过对三个不同的、广泛使用的时间序列数据集进行大量实验，我们证明我们的方法优于五种最先进的 CPD 方法，其中包括无监督和半监督方法。就三个数据集的平均 F1 分数而言，TS-CP^2 将使用手工统计或时间特征的方法的性能提高了 79.4%，将基于深度学习的方法的性能提高了 17.0%。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于自监督对比预测编码的时序变化点检测方法TS-CP²，通过学习时间间隔嵌入表示来提升检测性能。
AI.motivation: 现有变化点检测方法在处理复杂时序数据时存在特征提取能力不足、依赖人工设计特征或深度学习方法效果有限的问题，需更高效的自监督学习框架。
AI.method: 设计对比预测编码框架，通过分离相邻时间间隔嵌入与跨时间间隔嵌入的表示，利用自监督学习机制捕捉时序动态特征。
AI.result: 在三个公开时序数据集上，TS-CP²相较五种先进方法平均F1分数提升79.4%（手工特征方法）和17.0%（深度学习方法）。
AI.conclusion: TS-CP²验证了对比学习在时序变化点检测中的有效性，为无监督时序分析提供了新范式。

title: FreezeNet: Full Performance by Reduced Storage Costs
title_zh: FreezeNet：通过降低存储成本实现全面性能
comment: Conference Paper of the Asian Conference on Computer Vision (ACCV) 2020
comments: Conference Paper of the Asian Conference on Computer Vision (ACCV) 2020
abstract: Pruning generates sparse networks by setting parameters to zero. In this work we improve one-shot pruning methods, applied before training, without adding any additional storage costs while preserving the sparse gradient computations. The main difference to pruning is that we do not sparsify the network's weights but learn just a few key parameters and keep the other ones fixed at their random initialized value. This mechanism is called freezing the parameters. Those frozen weights can be stored efficiently with a single 32bit random seed number. The parameters to be frozen are determined one-shot by a single for- and backward pass applied before training starts. We call the introduced method FreezeNet. In our experiments we show that FreezeNets achieve good results, especially for extreme freezing rates. Freezing weights preserves the gradient flow throughout the network and consequently, FreezeNets train better and have an increased capacity compared to their pruned counterparts. On the classification tasks MNIST and CIFAR-10/100 we outperform SNIP, in this setting the best reported one-shot pruning method, applied before training. On MNIST, FreezeNet achieves 99.2% performance of the baseline LeNet-5-Caffe architecture, while compressing the number of trained and stored parameters by a factor of x 157.
summary: Pruning generates sparse networks by setting parameters to zero. In this work we improve one-shot pruning methods, applied before training, without adding any additional storage costs while preserving the sparse gradient computations. The main difference to pruning is that we do not sparsify the network's weights but learn just a few key parameters and keep the other ones fixed at their random initialized value. This mechanism is called freezing the parameters. Those frozen weights can be stored efficiently with a single 32bit random seed number. The parameters to be frozen are determined one-shot by a single for- and backward pass applied before training starts. We call the introduced method FreezeNet. In our experiments we show that FreezeNets achieve good results, especially for extreme freezing rates. Freezing weights preserves the gradient flow throughout the network and consequently, FreezeNets train better and have an increased capacity compared to their pruned counterparts. On the classification tasks MNIST and CIFAR-10/100 we outperform SNIP, in this setting the best reported one-shot pruning method, applied before training. On MNIST, FreezeNet achieves 99.2% performance of the baseline LeNet-5-Caffe architecture, while compressing the number of trained and stored parameters by a factor of x 157.
abstract_zh: 剪枝通过将参数设置为零来生成稀疏网络。在这项工作中，我们改进了在训练之前应用的一次性剪枝方法，在保留稀疏梯度计算的同时，不增加任何额外的存储成本。与剪枝的主要区别在于，我们不会稀疏网络的权重，而是仅学习几个关键参数，并将其他参数固定为随机初始化值。这种机制称为冻结参数。这些冻结的权重可以使用单个 32 位随机种子数进行有效存储。要冻结的参数是通过在训练开始之前应用的单个前向和后向传递一次性确定的。我们将引入的方法称为 FreezeNet。在我们的实验中，我们表明 FreezeNet 取得了良好的效果，特别是对于极端的冻结速率。冻结权重保留了整个网络的梯度流，因此，与修剪后的网络相比，FreezeNet 训练得更好，容量也更大。在分类任务 MNIST 和 CIFAR-10/100 上，我们优于 SNIP，在这种情况下，这是报告最好的一次性剪枝方法，在训练之前应用。在 MNIST 上，FreezeNet 实现了基线 LeNet-5-Caffe 架构 99.2% 的性能，同时将训练和存储参数的数量压缩了 157 倍。

AI.tldr: 本文提出FreezeNet方法，在不增加存储成本的前提下，通过冻结参数而非剪枝权重来减少参数数量并保持模型性能。
AI.motivation: 传统剪枝方法通过设置参数为零生成稀疏网络，但可能影响梯度流动。本文旨在以更低的存储成本实现高性能，通过冻结参数而非剪枝来保持网络容量。
AI.method: 在训练前通过单次前向和反向传播确定冻结参数，仅学习少量关键参数，其余参数固定为随机初始化值，并用32位随机种子高效存储冻结参数。
AI.result: 在MNIST和CIFAR-10/100数据集上，FreezeNet在极端冻结率下表现优于SNIP等现有方法，MNIST上达到基线LeNet-5-Caffe架构99.2%的性能，参数量减少157倍。
AI.conclusion: FreezeNet通过冻结参数机制有效平衡了存储成本与模型性能，在分类任务中展现出优于传统剪枝方法的潜力。

search_text: FreezeNet: Full Performance by Reduced Storage Costs FreezeNet：通过降低存储成本实现全面性能 Pruning generates sparse networks by setting parameters to zero. In this work we improve one-shot pruning methods, applied before training, without adding any additional storage costs while preserving the sparse gradient computations. The main difference to pruning is that we do not sparsify the network's weights but learn just a few key parameters and keep the other ones fixed at their random initialized value. This mechanism is called freezing the parameters. Those frozen weights can be stored efficiently with a single 32bit random seed number. The parameters to be frozen are determined one-shot by a single for- and backward pass applied before training starts. We call the introduced method FreezeNet. In our experiments we show that FreezeNets achieve good results, especially for extreme freezing rates. Freezing weights preserves the gradient flow throughout the network and consequently, FreezeNets train better and have an increased capacity compared to their pruned counterparts. On the classification tasks MNIST and CIFAR-10/100 we outperform SNIP, in this setting the best reported one-shot pruning method, applied before training. On MNIST, FreezeNet achieves 99.2% performance of the baseline LeNet-5-Caffe architecture, while compressing the number of trained and stored parameters by a factor of x 157. 剪枝通过将参数设置为零来生成稀疏网络。在这项工作中，我们改进了在训练之前应用的一次性剪枝方法，在保留稀疏梯度计算的同时，不增加任何额外的存储成本。与剪枝的主要区别在于，我们不会稀疏网络的权重，而是仅学习几个关键参数，并将其他参数固定为随机初始化值。这种机制称为冻结参数。这些冻结的权重可以使用单个 32 位随机种子数进行有效存储。要冻结的参数是通过在训练开始之前应用的单个前向和后向传递一次性确定的。我们将引入的方法称为 FreezeNet。在我们的实验中，我们表明 FreezeNet 取得了良好的效果，特别是对于极端的冻结速率。冻结权重保留了整个网络的梯度流，因此，与修剪后的网络相比，FreezeNet 训练得更好，容量也更大。在分类任务 MNIST 和 CIFAR-10/100 上，我们优于 SNIP，在这种情况下，这是报告最好的一次性剪枝方法，在训练之前应用。在 MNIST 上，FreezeNet 实现了基线 LeNet-5-Caffe 架构 99.2% 的性能，同时将训练和存储参数的数量压缩了 157 倍。 本文提出FreezeNet方法，在不增加存储成本的前提下，通过冻结参数而非剪枝权重来减少参数数量并保持模型性能。 Paul Wimmer,Jens Mehnert,Alexandru Condurache cs.LG,cs.CV

title: A Data-Driven Study of Commonsense Knowledge using the ConceptNet Knowledge Base
title_zh: 使用 ConceptNet 知识库进行常识知识的数据驱动研究
abstract: Acquiring commonsense knowledge and reasoning is recognized as an important frontier in achieving general Artificial Intelligence (AI). Recent research in the Natural Language Processing (NLP) community has demonstrated significant progress in this problem setting. Despite this progress, which is mainly on multiple-choice question answering tasks in limited settings, there is still a lack of understanding (especially at scale) of the nature of commonsense knowledge itself. In this paper, we propose and conduct a systematic study to enable a deeper understanding of commonsense knowledge by doing an empirical and structural analysis of the ConceptNet knowledge base. ConceptNet is a freely available knowledge base containing millions of commonsense assertions presented in natural language. Detailed experimental results on three carefully designed research questions, using state-of-the-art unsupervised graph representation learning ('embedding') and clustering techniques, reveal deep substructures in ConceptNet relations, allowing us to make data-driven and computational claims about the meaning of phenomena such as 'context' that are traditionally discussed only in qualitative terms. Furthermore, our methodology provides a case study in how to use data-science and computational methodologies for understanding the nature of an everyday (yet complex) psychological phenomenon that is an essential feature of human intelligence.
summary: Acquiring commonsense knowledge and reasoning is recognized as an important frontier in achieving general Artificial Intelligence (AI). Recent research in the Natural Language Processing (NLP) community has demonstrated significant progress in this problem setting. ConceptNet is a freely available knowledge base containing millions of commonsense assertions presented in natural language. Detailed experimental results on three carefully designed research questions, using state-of-the-art unsupervised graph representation learning ('embedding') and clustering techniques, reveal deep substructures in ConceptNet relations, allowing us to make data-driven and computational claims about the meaning of phenomena such as 'context' that are traditionally discussed only in qualitative terms. Furthermore, our methodology provides a case study in how to use data-science and computational methodologies for understanding the nature of an everyday (yet complex) psychological phenomenon that is an essential feature of human intelligence.
abstract_zh: 获取常识知识和推理被认为是实现通用人工智能（AI）的重要前沿。自然语言处理 (NLP) 社区的最新研究表明，在这个问题设置方面取得了重大进展。尽管取得了这些进展，主要是在有限环境下的多项选择题回答任务上，但人们仍然缺乏对常识知识本身性质的理解（尤其是在规模上）。在本文中，我们提出并进行了一项系统研究，通过对 ConceptNet 知识库进行实证和结构分析来更深入地理解常识知识。 ConceptNet 是一个免费的知识库，包含数以百万计的以自然语言呈现的常识性断言。针对三个精心设计的研究问题的详细实验结果，使用最先进的无监督图表示学习（“嵌入”）和聚类技术，揭示了 ConceptNet 关系中的深层子结构，使我们能够对传统上仅以定性术语讨论的“上下文”等现象的含义做出数据驱动和计算的主张。此外，我们的方法提供了一个案例研究，说明如何使用数据科学和计算方法来理解日常（但复杂）心理现象的本质，这是人类智力的基本特征。

AI.tldr: 本文通过分析ConceptNet知识库，深入研究常识知识的本质。
AI.motivation: 尽管在特定任务上取得进展，但对常识知识本身的性质缺乏大规模理解，需系统性研究。
AI.method: 使用无监督图表示学习和聚类技术，对ConceptNet进行实证与结构分析，设计三个研究问题进行实验。
AI.result: 揭示了ConceptNet关系的深层子结构，并对‘上下文’等现象提出数据驱动的计算性主张。
AI.conclusion: 展示了如何利用数据科学方法理解人类智能中的复杂心理现象，提供案例研究范式。

authors_text: Aaron Babier,Binghao Zhang,Rafid Mahmood,Kevin L. Moore,Thomas G. Purdie,Andrea L. McNiven,Timothy C. Y. Chan
title: OpenKBP: The open-access knowledge-based planning grand challenge
title_zh: OpenKBP：开放获取的基于知识的规划重大挑战
abstract: The purpose of this work is to advance fair and consistent comparisons of dose prediction methods for knowledge-based planning (KBP) in radiation therapy research. We hosted OpenKBP, a 2020 AAPM Grand Challenge, and challenged participants to develop the best method for predicting the dose of contoured CT images. The models were evaluated according to two separate scores: (1) dose score, which evaluates the full 3D dose distributions, and (2) dose-volume histogram (DVH) score, which evaluates a set DVH metrics. Participants were given the data of 340 patients who were treated for head-and-neck cancer with radiation therapy. The data was partitioned into training (n=200), validation (n=40), and testing (n=100) datasets. All participants performed training and validation with the corresponding datasets during the validation phase of the Challenge, and we ranked the models in the testing phase based on out-of-sample performance. The Challenge attracted 195 participants from 28 countries, and 73 of those participants formed 44 teams in the validation phase, which received a total of 1750 submissions. The testing phase garnered submissions from 28 teams. On average, over the course of the validation phase, participants improved the dose and DVH scores of their models by a factor of 2.7 and 5.7, respectively. In the testing phase one model achieved significantly better dose and DVH score than the runner-up models. Lastly, many of the top performing teams reported using generalizable techniques (e.g., ensembles) to achieve higher performance than their competition. This is the first competition for knowledge-based planning research, and it helped launch the first platform for comparing KBP prediction methods fairly and consistently. The OpenKBP datasets are available publicly to help benchmark future KBP research, which has also democratized KBP research by making it accessible to everyone.

abstract_zh: 这项工作的目的是促进放射治疗研究中基于知识的规划（KBP）的剂量预测方法的公平和一致的比较。我们主办了 2020 年 AAPM 大赛 OpenKBP，并向参与者发起挑战，要求他们开发预测轮廓 CT 图像剂量的最佳方法。这些模型根据两个单独的评分进行评估：(1) 剂量评分，用于评估完整的 3D 剂量分布；(2) 剂量体积直方图 (DVH) 评分，用于评估一组 DVH 指标。参与者获得了 340 名接受放射治疗的头颈癌患者的数据。数据分为训练数据集 (n=200)、验证数据集 (n=40) 和测试数据集 (n=100)。所有参与者在挑战赛的验证阶段都使用相应的数据集进行训练和验证，并且我们根据样本外性能对测试阶段的模型进行排名。此次挑战赛吸引了来自28个国家的195名参赛者，其中73名参赛者组成了44支队伍进入验证阶段，共收到1750份参赛作品。测试阶段收到了 28 个团队的提交意见。平均而言，在验证阶段，参与者将其模型的剂量和 DVH 评分分别提高了 2.7 和 5.7 倍。在测试阶段，第一模型的剂量和 DVH 得分明显优于第二模型。最后，许多表现最好的团队报告使用通用技术（例如集成）来实现比竞争对手更高的性能。这是首个基于知识的规划研究竞赛，它帮助推出了第一个公平、一致地比较 KBP 预测方法的平台。 OpenKBP 数据集可公开获取，以帮助对未来的 KBP 研究进行基准测试，这也通过让每个人都可以访问来实现 KBP 研究的民主化。

AI.tldr: 本文介绍了OpenKBP挑战赛，旨在通过公开数据集推动放射治疗中知识库计划（KBP）剂量预测方法的公平比较。
AI.motivation: 现有KBP研究缺乏统一的评估标准和公开数据集，限制了方法间的公平比较与技术普及。
AI.method: 举办OpenKBP挑战赛，提供340例头颈癌放疗患者的CT数据集（训练集200例，验证集40例，测试集100例），要求参赛者开发剂量预测模型，并通过3D剂量分布和DVH指标进行双维度评分。
AI.result: 吸引195名来自28国的研究者组成44支队伍，验证阶段模型剂量得分提升2.7倍、DVH得分提升5.7倍；测试阶段最优模型显著优于其他队伍，且多支队伍采用集成学习等通用技术提升性能。
AI.conclusion: OpenKBP首次建立KBP方法的标准化评估平台，通过公开数据集推动了该领域的技术民主化与研究进展。

ai_content.motivation: 现有KBP研究缺乏统一的评估标准和公开数据集，限制了方法间的公平比较与技术普及。
ai_content.method: 举办OpenKBP挑战赛，提供340例头颈癌放疗患者的CT数据集（训练集200例，验证集40例，测试集100例），要求参赛者开发剂量预测模型，并通过3D剂量分布和DVH指标进行双维度评分。
ai_content.result: 吸引195名来自28国的研究者组成44支队伍，验证阶段模型剂量得分提升2.7倍、DVH得分提升5.7倍；测试阶段最优模型显著优于其他队伍，且多支队伍采用集成学习等通用技术提升性能。
ai_content.conclusion: OpenKBP首次建立KBP方法的标准化评估平台，通过公开数据集推动了该领域的技术民主化与研究进展。

search_text: OpenKBP: The open-access knowledge-based planning grand challenge OpenKBP：开放获取的基于知识的规划重大挑战 The purpose of this work is to advance fair and consistent comparisons of dose prediction methods for knowledge-based planning (KBP) in radiation therapy research. We hosted OpenKBP, a 2020 AAPM Grand Challenge, and challenged participants to develop the best method for predicting the dose of contoured CT images. The models were evaluated according to two separate scores: (1) dose score, which evaluates the full 3D dose distributions, and (2) dose-volume histogram (DVH) score, which evaluates a set DVH metrics. Participants were given the data of 340 patients who were treated for head-and-neck cancer with radiation therapy. The data was partitioned into training (n=200), validation (n=40), and testing (n=100) datasets. All participants performed training and validation with the corresponding datasets during the validation phase of the Challenge, and we ranked the models in the testing phase based on out-of-sample performance. The Challenge attracted 195 participants from 28 countries, and 73 of those participants formed 44 teams in the validation phase, which received a total of 1750 submissions. The testing phase garnered submissions from 28 teams. On average, over the course of the validation phase, participants improved the dose and DVH scores of their models by a factor of 2.7 and 5.7, respectively. In the testing phase one model achieved significantly better dose and DVH score than the runner-up models. Lastly, many of the top performing teams reported using generalizable techniques (e.g., ensembles) to achieve higher performance than their competition. This is the first competition for knowledge-based planning research, and it helped launch the first platform for comparing KBP prediction methods fairly and consistently. The OpenKBP datasets are available publicly to help benchmark future KBP research, which has also democratized KBP research by making it accessible to everyone. 这项工作的目的是促进放射治疗研究中基于知识的规划（KBP）的剂量预测方法的公平和一致的比较。我们主办了 2020 年 AAPM 大赛 OpenKBP，并向参与者发起挑战，要求他们开发预测轮廓 CT 图像剂量的最佳方法。这些模型根据两个单独的评分进行评估：(1) 剂量评分，用于评估完整的 3D 剂量分布；(2) 剂量体积直方图 (DVH) 评分，用于评估一组 DVH 指标。参与者获得了 340 名接受放射治疗的头颈癌患者的数据。数据分为训练数据集 (n=200)、验证数据集 (n=40) 和测试数据集 (n=100)。所有参与者在挑战赛的验证阶段都使用相应的数据集进行训练和验证，并且我们根据样本外性能对测试阶段的模型进行排名。此次挑战赛吸引了来自28个国家的195名参赛者，其中73名参赛者组成了44支队伍进入验证阶段，共收到1750份参赛作品。测试阶段收到了 28 个团队的提交意见。平均而言，在验证阶段，参与者将其模型的剂量和 DVH 评分分别提高了 2.7 和 5.7 倍。在测试阶段，第一模型的剂量和 DVH 得分明显优于第二模型。最后，许多表现最好的团队报告使用通用技术（例如集成）来实现比竞争对手更高的性能。这是首个基于知识的规划研究竞赛，它帮助推出了第一个公平、一致地比较 KBP 预测方法的平台。 OpenKBP 数据集可公开获取，以帮助对未来的 KBP 研究进行基准测试，这也通过让每个人都可以访问来实现 KBP 研究的民主化。 本文介绍了OpenKBP挑战赛，旨在通过公开数据集推动放射治疗中知识库计划（KBP）剂量预测方法的公平比较。 Aaron Babier,Binghao Zhang,Rafid Mahmood,Kevin L. Moore,Thomas G. Purdie,Andrea L. McNiven,Timothy C. Y. Chan physics.med-ph,cs.CV

title: Feedback Effects in Repeat-Use Criminal Risk Assessments
title_zh: 重复使用犯罪风险评估中的反馈效应
comment: 10 pages. arXiv admin note: substantial text overlap with arXiv:2005.13404
comments: 10 pages. arXiv admin note: substantial text overlap with arXiv:2005.13404
abstract: In the criminal legal context, risk assessment algorithms are touted as data-driven, well-tested tools. Studies known as validation tests are typically cited by practitioners to show that a particular risk assessment algorithm has predictive accuracy, establishes legitimate differences between risk groups, and maintains some measure of group fairness in treatment. To establish these important goals, most tests use a one-shot, single-point measurement. Using a Polya Urn model, we explore the implication of feedback effects in sequential scoring-decision processes. We show through simulation that risk can propagate over sequential decisions in ways that are not captured by one-shot tests. For example, even a very small or undetectable level of bias in risk allocation can amplify over sequential risk-based decisions, leading to observable group differences after a number of decision iterations. Risk assessment tools operate in a highly complex and path-dependent process, fraught with historical inequity. We conclude from this study that these tools do not properly account for compounding effects, and require new approaches to development and auditing.
summary: In the criminal legal context, risk assessment algorithms are touted as data-driven, well-tested tools. Studies known as validation tests are typically cited by practitioners to show that a particular risk assessment algorithm has predictive accuracy, establishes legitimate differences between risk groups, and maintains some measure of group fairness in treatment. To establish these important goals, most tests use a one-shot, single-point measurement. Using a Polya Urn model, we explore the implication of feedback effects in sequential scoring-decision processes. We show through simulation that risk can propagate over sequential decisions in ways that are not captured by one-shot tests. For example, even a very small or undetectable level of bias in risk allocation can amplify over sequential risk-based decisions, leading to observable group differences after a number of decision iterations. Risk assessment tools operate in a highly complex and path-dependent process, fraught with historical inequity. We conclude from this study that these tools do not properly account for compounding effects, and require new approaches to development and auditing.
abstract_zh: 在刑事法律背景下，风险评估算法被吹捧为数据驱动、经过充分测试的工具。从业者通常引用称为验证测试​​的研究来表明特定的风险评估算法具有预测准确性，建立风险组之间的合理差异，并在治疗中保持某种群体公平性的措施。为了建立这些重要目标，大多数测试都使用一次性单点测量。使用 Polya Urn 模型，我们探索了反馈效应在顺序评分决策过程中的含义。我们通过模拟表明，风险可以通过一次性测试无法捕获的方式在连续决策中传播。例如，即使风险分配中的偏差非常小或无法检测到，也可能会放大基于风险的连续决策，导致在多次决策迭代后出现可观察到的群体差异。风险评估工具的运作过程高度复杂且依赖于路径，充满了历史上的不平等。我们从这项研究中得出的结论是，这些工具没有正确考虑复合效应，需要新的开发和审计方法。

AI.tldr: 本文研究重复使用刑事风险评估工具时反馈效应的影响，揭示单次验证测试未能捕捉的累积偏差问题。
AI.motivation: 现有风险评估算法依赖单次验证测试，但实际决策过程中反馈效应可能导致偏差累积，加剧群体差异。
AI.method: 采用波利亚 urn 模型进行模拟实验，分析序列决策中风险分配的反馈机制。
AI.result: 模拟显示，即使初始偏差极小，也会在多次决策中放大，导致显著的群体差异。
AI.conclusion: 当前风险评估工具未充分考虑累积效应，需开发新的评估与审计方法以应对路径依赖问题。

authors_text: Declan McIntosh,Tunai Porto Marques,Alexandra Branzan Albu,Rodney Rountree,Fabio De Leo
title: Movement Tracks for the Automatic Detection of Fish Behavior in Videos
title_zh: 自动检测视频中鱼类行为的运动轨迹
comment: 8 pages, To be published in NeurIPS 2020 Workshop Tackling Climate Change with Machine Learning
comments: 8 pages, To be published in NeurIPS 2020 Workshop Tackling Climate Change with Machine Learning
abstract: Global warming is predicted to profoundly impact ocean ecosystems. Fish behavior is an important indicator of changes in such marine environments. Thus, the automatic identification of key fish behavior in videos represents a much needed tool for marine researchers, enabling them to study climate change-related phenomena. We offer a dataset of sablefish (Anoplopoma fimbria) startle behaviors in underwater videos, and investigate the use of deep learning (DL) methods for behavior detection on it. Our proposed detection system identifies fish instances using DL-based frameworks, determines trajectory tracks, derives novel behavior-specific features, and employs Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks to identify startle behavior in sablefish. Its performance is studied by comparing it with a state-of-the-art DL-based video event detector.
summary: Global warming is predicted to profoundly impact ocean ecosystems. Fish behavior is an important indicator of changes in such marine environments. Thus, the automatic identification of key fish behavior in videos represents a much needed tool for marine researchers, enabling them to study climate change-related phenomena. We offer a dataset of sablefish (Anoplopoma fimbria) startle behaviors in underwater videos, and investigate the use of deep learning (DL) methods for behavior detection on it. Our proposed detection system identifies fish instances using DL-based frameworks, determines trajectory tracks, derives novel behavior-specific features, and employs Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks to identify startle behavior in sablefish. Its performance is studied by comparing it with a state-of-the-art DL-based video event detector.
abstract_zh: 预计全球变暖将深刻影响海洋生态系统。鱼类行为是此类海洋环境变化的重要指标。因此，自动识别视频中关键鱼类行为是海洋研究人员急需的工具，使他们能够研究与气候变化相关的现象。我们提供了水下视频中黑貂鱼（Anoplopoma fimbria）惊吓行为的数据集，并研究了使用深度学习（DL）方法对其进行行为检测。我们提出的检测系统使用基于深度学习的框架来识别鱼类实例，确定轨迹轨迹，导出新颖的行为特定特征，并采用长短期记忆（LSTM）网络来识别黑貂鱼的惊吓行为。通过将其与最先进的基于深度学习的视频事件检测器进行比较来研究其性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于深度学习和运动轨迹分析的自动检测鱼类惊吓行为的系统，用于海洋环境变化研究。
AI.motivation: 全球变暖导致海洋生态系统变化，鱼类行为是重要环境指标，需自动化工具辅助研究气候相关现象。
AI.method: 采用深度学习框架识别鱼实例，通过轨迹跟踪提取行为特征，结合LSTM网络检测惊吓行为，并与先进方法对比验证。
AI.result: 系统在sablefish惊吓行为检测任务中表现优于现有视频事件检测方法，验证了方法有效性。
AI.conclusion: 该系统为海洋研究提供自动化工具，未来可扩展至其他鱼类行为检测，助力气候影响分析。

We offer a dataset of sablefish (Anoplopoma fimbria) startle behaviors in underwater videos, and investigate the use of deep learning (DL) methods for behavior detection on it. Our proposed detection system identifies fish instances using DL-based frameworks, determines trajectory tracks, derives novel behavior-specific features, and employs Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks to identify startle behavior in sablefish. Its performance is studied by comparing it with a state-of-the-art DL-based video event detector. 预计全球变暖将深刻影响海洋生态系统。鱼类行为是此类海洋环境变化的重要指标。因此，自动识别视频中关键鱼类行为是海洋研究人员急需的工具，使他们能够研究与气候变化相关的现象。我们提供了水下视频中黑貂鱼（Anoplopoma fimbria）惊吓行为的数据集，并研究了使用深度学习（DL）方法对其进行行为检测。我们提出的检测系统使用基于深度学习的框架来识别鱼类实例，确定轨迹轨迹，导出新颖的行为特定特征，并采用长短期记忆（LSTM）网络来识别黑貂鱼的惊吓行为。通过将其与最先进的基于深度学习的视频事件检测器进行比较来研究其性能。 本文提出一种基于深度学习和运动轨迹分析的自动检测鱼类惊吓行为的系统，用于海洋环境变化研究。 Declan McIntosh,Tunai Porto Marques,Alexandra Branzan Albu,Rodney Rountree,Fabio De Leo cs.CV

title: Overview of Screen Content Coding in Recently Developed Video Coding Standards
title_zh: 最近开发的视频编码标准中的屏幕内容编码概述
abstract: In recent years, screen content (SC) video including computer generated text, graphics and animations, have drawn more attention than ever, as many related applications become very popular. To address the need for efficient coding of such contents, a number of coding tools have been specifically developed and achieved great advances in terms of coding efficiency. The inclusion of screen content coding (SCC) features in all the recently developed video coding standards (namely, HEVC SCC, VVC, AVS3, AV1 and EVC) demonstrated the importance of supporting such features. This paper provides an overview and comparative study of screen content coding technologies, with discussions on the performance and complexity aspects for the tools developed in these standards.
summary: In recent years, screen content (SC) video including computer generated text, graphics and animations, have drawn more attention than ever, as many related applications become very popular. To address the need for efficient coding of such contents, a number of coding tools have been specifically developed and achieved great advances in terms of coding efficiency. The inclusion of screen content coding (SCC) features in all the recently developed video coding standards (namely, HEVC SCC, VVC, AVS3, AV1 and EVC) demonstrated the importance of supporting such features. This paper provides an overview and comparative study of screen content coding technologies, with discussions on the performance and complexity aspects for the tools developed in these standards.
abstract_zh: 近年来，随着许多相关应用程序变得非常流行，包括计算机生成的文本、图形和动画在内的屏幕内容（SC）视频比以往任何时候都吸引了更多的关注。为了满足对此类内容进行高效编码的需求，专门开发了许多编码工具，并在编码效率方面取得了巨大进步。最近开发的所有视频编码标准（即 HEVC SCC、VVC、AVS3、AV1 和 EVC）中都包含屏幕内容编码 (SCC) 功能，这证明了支持此类功能的重要性。本文提供了屏幕内容编码技术的概述和比较研究，并讨论了这些标准中开发的工具的性能和复杂性方面。

AI.tldr: 本文综述了近年来视频编码标准中屏幕内容编码技术的发展，并对比分析了相关工具的性能与复杂度。
AI.motivation: 随着计算机生成文本、图形和动画等屏幕内容视频应用的普及，亟需高效编码工具以满足压缩效率需求，推动相关技术纳入最新视频编码标准。
AI.method: 通过对比分析HEVC SCC、VVC、AVS3、AV1和EVC等标准中的屏幕内容编码工具，讨论其性能表现与实现复杂度。
AI.result: 明确了各标准编码工具在压缩效率和复杂度上的差异，验证了屏幕内容编码技术对提升视频压缩性能的关键作用。
AI.conclusion: 屏幕内容编码已成为现代视频编码标准的核心组成部分，未来需进一步优化工具设计以平衡效率与实现成本。
